target.title: fix(sessions): honor explicit default model pins
target.body:
## Summary
- Persist explicit user selections of the configured default model as exact session model pins instead of clearing them as if they were unpinned defaults.
- Keep `/model default`, `session_status(model=default)`, and `sessions.patch model:null` as the explicit clear/unpin paths.
- Carry the exact selection through reply, queued-run, agent-command, reset, status, and sessions.patch paths so channel/parent/config fallback policy cannot silently replace user intent.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Explicit `/model <configured default>` selections no longer lose user intent just because they match the configured primary; fallback policy treats them as strict user session selections, while `/model default` still clears back to unpinned behavior.
Real environment tested: Local beatrice.local source checkout at PR head `98ca4cd8b150ee0be7fcd602a01c4aed346f316c` on May 30, 2026. Ran a local source-module behavior probe with `node --import tsx` against the current checkout; no channel credentials, provider API keys, live runtime files, or LaunchAgent state were used or modified.
Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'EOF' ... EOF` (local source-module behavior probe that applied an explicit configured-default selection, changed the configured primary, rendered `/status`, applied `/model default`, and rendered `/status` again)
2. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/sessions/model-overrides.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts src/commands/status.summary.test.ts`
3. `git diff --check`

target.title: refactor: route auto-reply sessions through session seam
target.body:
## What

Path 3 / PR 3.1b auto-reply/agent: routes auto-reply and agent runtime session writes through the 3.1a session accessor seam while preserving current file-backed behavior. Refs #88838.

## Why

This moves the auto-reply/agent slice off direct session-store mutations before the SQLite storage flip, keeping caller adoption separate from storage implementation changes. The PR depends on the 3.1a seam base branch `clawdbot-9c3/session-accessor-seam` at `be13ad13f8` and its seam commits including `bb48e18ded`, `f6ae83451a`, `0faccc0cfa`, `5c38bb4be4`, `2d9c5b65a3`, and `be13ad13f8`.

## Changes

- Route agent runtime session writes through seam
- Route auto-reply session updates through seam
- Preserve file-backed behavior and current shapes
- Keep transcript/file-semantic callers out of scope
- No storage flip, runtime dual-read, or SQLite schema work
- No blockers

| Area | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/agents/command/*` | Moves command/session persistence to seam helpers |
| `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/*` | Routes embedded runtime session mutations through seam |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/*` | Moves auto-reply run/session state updates to seam |

## Testing

- Source branch `clawdbot-6f0/31b-auto-reply-agent-seam` at `582bdb04e7` (`7bd273b08c` + `582bdb04e7` on the 3.1a base).
- File-backed proof on the source branch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs` over agent command/session and auto-reply runner/session tests passed 428 tests.
- Disposable SQLite validation branch `integration/sqlite-flip-auto-reply-agent` at `b1600517d6`.
- SQLite validation proof passed 439 tests across the same slice plus `src/config/sessions/session-accessor.conformance.test.ts` and `src/auto-reply/reply/sqlite-session-accessor.validation.test.ts`.
- Validation branch cherry-picked `3b9353414e` (3.1a read-scope fix), `59a590e825` (3.2 SQLite foundation), and `c221e48beb` (3.2 read-scope fix) with no storage fallback or dual-read wiring.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/config/sessions/session-accessor.ts src/auto-reply/reply/sqlite-session-accessor.validation.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/config/sessions/session-accessor.ts src/auto-reply/reply/sqlite-session-accessor.validation.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: PR #89124 routes auto-reply sessions through the session-accessor seam after the #90463 rebase, preserving the narrow auto-reply slice.

Real environment tested: Managed live OpenClaw Gateway on this machine, running PR head f0a985fa9b484079ef81f261c5d1431835320a10 after pnpm build and LaunchAgent Gateway restart. The CLI reported OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (f0a985f), the Gateway was listening on loopback port 18789, and the agent used the live openai/gpt-5.5 provider.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran pnpm openclaw agent against the managed Gateway with session key agent:main:pr89124-live-gateway-proof-20260615T2017Z and prompt "Reply exactly with PR89124_LIVE_OK and nothing else." Then ran delivered channel proofs with pnpm openclaw agent --deliver for the configured Discord channel and configured Telegram group topic.

Evidence after fix: Gateway run 54cde5c1-d306-4a71-80eb-3d8a8b18d1b6 completed in session 5676b0d9-3548-4b64-aa4a-2df6dcb571bd with output PR89124_LIVE_OK; the persisted session row reported status done, modelProvider openai, model gpt-5.5, totalTokensFresh true, and a transcript file exists. Discord delivery run 465d5103-b947-4500-ad0c-584a90f8526e completed in session 6e8d619d-10c7-4bef-b866-4f172ed68ee2 with payload PR89124_DISCORD_OK., deliveryStatus sent, succeeded true. Telegram delivery run d575b759-5865-47a4-a5f6-3776ecd99dce completed in session 6a240460-5e6b-48c0-a52d-207f5fcc1f6d with payload PR89124_TELEGRAM_OK, deliveryStatus sent, succeeded true.

Observed result after fix: The real managed Gateway accepted the PR build, completed the Gateway agent turn, and sent real outbound replies through both the Discord and Telegram channel adapters.

What was not tested: I did not prove inbound human-authored Discord or Telegram trigger messages in this pass; the channel proof used Gateway-delivered outbound replies to already configured live channel targets.

target.title: fix(plugin-runtime): restore channel turn alias
target.body:
## Summary

- Restores the deprecated `core.channel.turn.*` runtime surface as an alias of `core.channel.inbound.*` for shipped channel plugins compiled before the inbound rename.
- Keeps the alias as the same object as `inbound`, so there is no second implementation path or drift in production runtime behavior.
- Updates the PluginRuntime type, plugin runtime mock, API baseline hash, and SDK docs to describe the compatibility alias while keeping new plugin guidance on `inbound.*`.
- Intentionally does not change bundled Feishu source, channel logic, config, or package artifacts.

## Linked context

Closes #91122

Related #91122

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No direct maintainer request; this is a focused compatibility fix for the reported shipped plugin runtime break.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Restores `runtime.channel.turn.*` / `core.channel.turn.*` as a deprecated compatibility alias for old channel plugins while preserving `runtime.channel.inbound.*` as the canonical API.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, Node 24-compatible repo toolchain, branch `fix/91122-runtime-channel-turn-alias` after this patch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built the runtime, then ran this Node probe against the generated `dist/runtime-channel-UUbgZd8n.js` chunk:

```console
$ node --input-type=module - <<'NODE'
import { createRuntimeChannel } from './dist/runtime-channel-UUbgZd8n.js';
const channel = createRuntimeChannel({
  providerControl: {},
  createUnifiedProviderSetup: () => ({}),
  ensureRuntimeAuthDeps: () => ({}),
  runtimeAdapters: {},
  createAgentStateDB: () => ({}),
  createAgentStateDBForStorePath: () => ({}),
  registerInboundSession: () => {},
  runWithDispatch: () => {},
});
console.log(JSON.stringify({
  hasTurn: Boolean(channel.turn),
  sameObject: channel.turn === channel.inbound,
  sameRun: channel.turn.run === channel.inbound.run,
  sameBuildContext: channel.turn.buildContext === channel.inbound.buildContext,
  sameDispatchReply: channel.turn.dispatchReply === channel.inbound.dispatchReply,
  sameRunPreparedReply: channel.turn.runPreparedReply === channel.inbound.runPreparedReply,
}));
NODE
```

Evidence after fix:

```console
{"hasTurn":true,"sameObject":true,"sameRun":true,"sameBuildContext":true,"sameDispatchReply":true,"sameRunPreparedReply":true}
```

Observed result after fix: The built runtime exposes `channel.turn`; `channel.turn === channel.inbound`; all four legacy functions are the same function references as the canonical inbound runtime helpers.

What was not tested: I did not install `openclaw@2026.6.x` with `@openclaw/feishu@2026.5.28` or run a live Feishu DM against real credentials.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof validates the exact built runtime compatibility surface that old compiled plugins dereference. It does not prove a full Feishu WebSocket setup because that requires live Feishu credentials and an older published plugin install.

Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Issue #91122 reports old plugins failing on `core.channel.turn.run()` because the runtime only exposed `channel.inbound.*`.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/runtime/runtime-channel.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.artifacts/vitest-cache-91122 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:gen`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `pnpm format:docs:check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugins/runtime/types-channel.ts src/plugins/runtime/runtime-channel.ts src/plugins/runtime/runtime-channel.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.ts src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/ui.js build`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

Regression coverage added or updated:

- `src/plugins/runtime/runtime-channel.test.ts` now asserts deprecated `channel.turn` is wired to `channel.inbound` with object/function identity.
- `src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/plugin-runtime-mock.test.ts` now asserts the test helper exposes the same alias and that inbound overrides are visible through `turn`.

What failed before this fix, if known:

- Old compiled plugins calling `core.channel.turn.run()` could dereference `undefined.run` after the runtime rename removed the `turn` key.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added for the runtime surface and the SDK runtime mock.

Notes:

- A full `pnpm build` rerun reached `tsdown`, runtime postbuild, plugin SDK DTS generation, SDK export checks, and the final `ui:build` stage, then hit my 15 minute local command timeout before completion. I ran the final UI build stage separately with `node scripts/ui.js build`, which passed. An earlier full `pnpm build` completed successfully on this worktree before the final test-helper alias correction.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes, old shipped channel plugins can call the deprecated runtime alias again.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Plugin runtime compatibility surface and SDK typing.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The alias reuses the exact same object as `channel.inbound`, avoiding duplicate behavior.
- Type/API baseline, focused runtime tests, plugin runtime mock tests, docs, DTS strict smoke, and autoreview all cover the changed surface.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Ready for ClawSweeper and maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI and ClawSweeper review are pending after the latest branch and proof-body update. Full live Feishu old-plugin validation was not run locally.

commands.author: BSG2000

target.title: fix(ui): show session name in browser title
target.body:
## Summary
- Updates the Control UI browser tab title to include the active session name, for example `Release triage - OpenClaw Control`.
- Keeps the static `OpenClaw Control` title when no session is selected.
- Syncs the title when the active session changes or when refreshed session metadata updates labels/display names.

## Linked context
Which issue does this close?
Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?
None.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?
No. This came from user feedback while working with multiple parallel OpenClaw sessions where every tab showed the same `OpenClaw Control` title.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: Browser tabs for different Control UI sessions were indistinguishable because every tab used the static `OpenClaw Control` document title.
- Real environment tested: Control UI unit/runtime helpers in the OpenClaw repo on Windows, using the UI Vitest project and production UI build.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```text
  node scripts\\run-vitest.mjs run ui\\src\\ui\\document-title.node.test.ts ui\\src\\ui\\app-lifecycle.node.test.ts
  cd ui && npm run build
  ```
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  ```text
  ui/src/ui/document-title.node.test.ts (6 tests) passed
  ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.node.test.ts (8 tests) passed
  Tests 14 passed (14)
  vite build: 919 modules transformed, built successfully
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The title resolver produces session-specific titles like `Release triage - OpenClaw Control`, and lifecycle sync updates `document.title` when `sessionKey` or `sessionsResult` changes.
- What was not tested: A manual browser screenshot was not captured.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This is a UI behavior change validated with targeted UI tests and production build rather than a live browser recording.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The static HTML title remains `<title>OpenClaw Control</title>`, and runtime code previously did not update `document.title` for the active session.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```text
node scripts\\run-vitest.mjs run ui\\src\\ui\\document-title.node.test.ts ui\\src\\ui\\app-lifecycle.node.test.ts
cd ui && npm run build
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added `document-title.node.test.ts` coverage for label, display name, fallback, and `document.title` sync behavior. Existing lifecycle tests were run to cover the integration point.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Parallel Control UI tabs all kept the static browser title `OpenClaw Control`, making sessions hard to distinguish.

If no test was added, why not?

A regression test was added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)
Yes. Browser tab titles now include the active session name.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)
No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)
No.

What is the highest-risk area?

The highest-risk area is ensuring the document title stays in sync with session metadata without affecting chat state persistence.

How is that risk mitigated?

The change is isolated to a document-title helper and lifecycle sync on `sessionKey`/`sessionsResult` changes, with targeted tests plus an unchanged passing production build.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Waiting on maintainer review and CI.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

None yet.

target.title: fix(discord): block cross-provider guild admin actions
target.body:
Summary

Fixes Discord guild-admin message action authorization so privileged Discord admin actions require a trusted Discord requester identity when invoked from another channel provider, while preserving trusted no-context owner/manual Discord admin actions.

Changes

- Centralize the Discord privileged guild-admin action list in a shared helper.
- Require trusted requester identity for privileged Discord actions in tool contexts before shared dispatch enters the Discord handler.
- Reject explicit non-Discord origins in the Discord guild-admin handler instead of dropping requester identity and continuing through a no-sender path.
- Preserve no-context owner/manual Discord guild-admin actions through the shared runner.
- Keep read-only Discord guild lookups available from non-Discord requesters.

Validation

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts discord/src/actions/handle-action.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts discord/src --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.ts extensions/discord/src/actions/handle-action.ts extensions/discord/src/channel-actions.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Cross-provider attempts to run privileged Discord guild-admin message actions are rejected unless they originate with a trusted Discord sender identity, and trusted owner/manual no-context Discord admin actions remain available.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `747` at committed head `d858762258db25478a0874a9701122ea69a7b626`, configured with a real Telegram bot token, real approved Telegram sender, real Discord bot token, and a throwaway Discord server/channel. Telegram token, Discord token, sender id, guild id, and channel id are redacted. No fetch stubs, mocks, or test-only Discord/Telegram transport overrides were used for this live cross-provider proof.

Exact real setup steps after this patch:

- `pnpm openclaw channels add --channel discord --token <redacted>` configured the real Discord bot.
- `pnpm openclaw channels capabilities --channel discord` confirmed the bot account was reachable through Discord API and listed Discord guild admin actions.
- `pnpm openclaw channels add --channel telegram --token <redacted>` configured the real Telegram bot.
- `pnpm openclaw channels capabilities --channel telegram` confirmed the Telegram bot account was reachable.
- `pnpm openclaw pairing approve telegram <redacted-pairing-code>` approved the real Telegram sender and configured it as command owner.
- `pnpm openclaw gateway run --dev --force --auth none --verbose --allow-unconfigured --bind loopback` started the gateway with Discord and Telegram plugins loaded.
- A disposable Discord channel named `openclaw-cross-provider-<redacted>` was created in a throwaway server through Discord API.
- The approved Telegram user sent the live inbound message: `Delete Discord channel <redacted-disposable-channel-id>`.
- A direct Discord API read of the same channel id after the OpenClaw action returned HTTP `200`, confirming the channel still existed after the blocked action.
- The disposable Discord channel was then cleaned up directly through Discord API; a final read returned HTTP `404`.

Live Telegram-to-Discord evidence after fix:

```text
telegram inbound: chatId=<redacted> from=telegram:<redacted> preview="... Delete Discord channel <redacted-disposable-channel-id>"
[ws] res ✗ message.action errorCode=UNAVAILABLE errorMessage=Error: Discord guild admin actions require a trusted Discord sender identity. channel=discord
Discord API channel read after blocked OpenClaw action: HTTP 200
Discord API cleanup delete: HTTP 200
Discord API cleanup verification read: HTTP 404
```

Observed result after fix: the live Telegram-origin request reached the OpenClaw gateway, entered the agent run, attempted a Discord `message.action`, and failed closed with `Discord guild admin actions require a trusted Discord sender identity.` The Discord channel still existed afterward, proving the cross-provider privileged Discord delete did not mutate Discord.

Preserved trusted owner/manual Discord proof: on the same committed head, a real `runMessageAction` call with `action: "channel-delete"`, `channel: "discord"`, the disposable Discord channel id, and `senderIsOwner: true` reached the Discord plugin through OpenClaw and performed a real Discord channel deletion. A direct Discord API read of that deleted channel then returned HTTP `404`.

Preserved owner/manual evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "kind": "action",
  "channel": "discord",
  "action": "channel-delete",
  "handledBy": "plugin",
  "dryRun": false,
  "payload": {
    "ok": true,
    "channelId": "<redacted-disposable-channel-id>"
  }
}
```

Test evidence after fix: the runner dispatch test passed with 1 test file and 23 tests, and the Discord extension lane passed with 158 test files and 1766 tests.

Notes

- `CHANGELOG.md` was not updated.

target.title: fix(ui): restore provider usage pill in desktop chat composer [AI]
target.body: ## Summary After 2026.6.6 the provider usage/quota pill (5h + weekly limits) disappeared from the desktop Control UI chat. The composer refactors (`feat: calm composer controls #88772`, `feat(webui): add session workspace rail #92856`) moved the desktop chat controls into `renderChatControls` but dropped the quota pill; the only desktop caller that still rendered it (the `renderChatSessionSelect` wrapper) became orphaned, so the pill rendered nowhere on desktop. Mobile was unaffected. This restores the **existing** pill (`renderChatQuotaPill`) into `renderChatControls` using the existing `models.authStatus` usage-window contract — provider-agnostic, no new config, no Codex/protocol change. It is intentionally scoped to re-attaching the existing pill, not the separately-tracked always-visible topbar. The pill now sits in the composer controls row (next to the model selector) rather than the pre-2026.6.6 upper-right header, since that desktop header no longer exists. Surface: `ui/` (Control UI desktop chat composer controls). Root cause / fix details: - `renderChatControls` (the shipped desktop composer controls) rendered the model select + settings popover but no longer the quota pill. - `renderGuardedChatControls` wraps `renderChatControls` in `guard([...])`; the dep list omitted `state.modelAuthStatusResult`, so even once the pill is re-attached the guarded controls would not re-render when usage windows arrive async. Added that dependency. - Desktop `.agent-chat__composer-controls` is flex (pill fits); the `@media (max-width: 768px)` layout is a fixed 2-column grid (model | settings), so the pill is hidden on mobile to avoid pushing the settings chip onto a second row. ## Linked context Refs #93041 ## Real behavior proof - **Behavior addressed:** Desktop Control UI chat no longer shows the OpenAI/Codex (or any monitored provider) usage pill after 2026.6.6; this restores it and makes it update when `models.authStatus` usage windows arrive. - **Real environment tested:** The production Control UI, served by the mocked-gateway harness and rendered in **real headless Chromium 148** (Playwright) at a 1280×900 desktop viewport — the full app on the real `/chat` route, not jsdom. `models.authStatus` returned an OAuth Codex provider with 5h + Week usage windows. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui-e2e.config.ts --configLoader runner ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-quota-pill-93041.e2e.test.ts` - **Evidence after fix:** Headless Chromium screenshot of the production Control UI desktop chat — the composer controls row shows the restored provider pill reading **"Usage 29%"**, linking to `/usage`, between the model selector and the Chat settings button. - **Observed result after fix:** The pill renders **"Usage 29%"** in the desktop composer controls and links to `/usage`; it is absent when no provider usage windows are present. On `origin/main`, the identical harness rendered no pill even with usage windows present. - **What was not tested:** No live in-browser session against a real ChatGPT/Codex OAuth subscription (the gateway `models.authStatus` was mocked with representative OAuth usage windows). Mobile intentionally keeps no composer pill (hidden via CSS to preserve the 2-column mobile grid). ## Tests and validation - `ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-quota-pill-93041.e2e.test.ts` — **real-browser (Playwright/Chromium)** test: mocks `models.authStatus` usage windows, asserts the pill renders in `.agent-chat__composer-controls` on `/chat`, and that it is absent without usage windows. Skips gracefully when Chromium is unavailable. 2/2 pass. - `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.browser.test.ts` — added 2 jsdom tests asserting the pill renders in `renderChatControls` with usage data and is absent without it (guards the live surface the existing `chat.test.ts` test missed — it rendered the orphaned wrapper). 9/9 pass. - `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` — 104/104, unchanged. - `oxfmt --check`, `oxlint`, `tsgo:core`, `tsgo:test:ui` — all clean. ## Risk Low, UI-only. Reuses an existing render helper and the existing `models.authStatus` projection; no config/default/migration/auth-logic change. ## AI assistance AI-assisted (Claude). Investigation, fix, and proof were reviewed and are owned by me.

target.title: fix(feishu): carry forward DM fallback and topic labels
target.body:
## Summary

Carry forward the remaining Feishu display-name behavior from #38958 after #51032 already landed the group-name session label fix.

This replacement should stay narrow:
- keep #51032's group-name implementation as the baseline
- add/repair DM display-name fallback through chatMembers with contact fallback
- preserve resolveSenderNames opt-out semantics
- scope sender/direct-member caches by account and relevant chat/sender identifiers
- surface topic/root labels only for topic-scoped Feishu sessions
- propagate permission guidance when DM fallback lookup hits a Feishu permission error

target.title: [AI-assisted] Persist agent-run error replies in chat history
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: A started Control UI agent run could surface a terminal provider/runtime error live, but `chat.history` after refresh did not retain an assistant error turn for that failed run.
- Solution: Treat agent-run failure replies as real error payloads and make Gateway persist exactly one display-safe assistant error transcript entry for returned agent errors, even when the runtime emitted only an internal error stub.
- What changed: Runner failure payloads are marked `isError`, Gateway appends a deduped `<runId>:assistant-error` transcript entry for returned agent errors, and regression coverage now locks the returned-error, runtime-stub, and duplicate/idempotency paths.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No provider behavior changes, no delivery routing changes, no config/lifecycle changes, no raw transcript writes, and no dependency or lockfile changes.

## Motivation

- A user should not lose the visible assistant error after refreshing the Control UI. If a started run fails before normal assistant output, the live error event and durable history should agree.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #89197
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: A real Gateway/WebSocket Control UI run that fails at provider transport now leaves exactly one assistant error in refreshed `chat.history`.
- Real environment tested: Disposable Linux OpenClaw checkout with the patched PR branch, real Gateway server startup, real WebSocket RPC client, channels skipped with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, and an intentionally unreachable OpenAI-compatible base URL to trigger the provider failure without secrets.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts -- -t 'assistant error|returned agent-run error payloads|runtime emitted an internal error stub'
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.error-broadcast.test.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts -- -t 'Agent failed before reply|assistant error|fallback candidate|error stub'
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node --import tsx ../real-chat-history-proof.ts after-gateway-force-v3
/usr/bin/time -v node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
Real runtime proof:
- provider transport failed with fetch failed / Connection error
- agent.wait status: error
- immediateHistoryCount: 3
- refreshHistoryCount: 3
- immediateAssistantCount: 1
- refreshAssistantCount: 1
- chatErrorEventCount: 1
- result: ONE_ASSISTANT_ERROR_IN_REFRESHED_HISTORY

Focused Gateway proof: 3 passed, 102 skipped.
Adjacent Gateway proof: 107 passed.
Session tool result guard proof: 35 passed.
Runner/Followup focused proof: 8 passed, 214 skipped.
git diff --check: clean.
```

- Observed result after fix: The live provider failure still broadcasts the expected chat error, and both immediate and post-reconnect `chat.history` contain one assistant error text: `Agent failed before reply: LLM request failed: network connection error.`
- What was not tested: Full repository-wide test suite, successful `tsgo` core on this testserver, real provider credentials, and a browser screenshot.
- Before evidence:

```text
Unpatched main proof for the same real Gateway/WebSocket/provider-failure path:
- sendStatus: started
- agent.wait status: ok
- immediateHistoryCount: 2
- refreshHistoryCount: 2
- immediateAssistantCount: 0
- refreshAssistantCount: 0
- chatErrorEventCount: 0
- result: ASSISTANT_ERROR_HISTORY_MISMATCH
```

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Runner final failure replies were not consistently marked as error payloads, and Gateway treated the runtime assistant-error callback as if it guaranteed a Control UI-visible `chat.history` assistant turn.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Previous coverage asserted live error broadcast behavior but did not prove refreshed `chat.history` retained an assistant error after a started real runtime failure.
- Contributing context (if known): Runtime/session guard error stubs can be internal transcript-only markers; Gateway still needs to durably mirror returned terminal agent errors into the Control UI history path.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`, and the temporary real Gateway/WebSocket proof harness.
- Scenario the test should lock in: A started run returns an agent error; Gateway appends one assistant error transcript entry, keeps the live error terminal event, survives reconnect/history refresh, and does not duplicate the assistant error.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The source tests cover the exact Gateway and runner seams, while the real proof exercises server startup, WebSocket RPC, provider transport failure, `agent.wait`, and refreshed `chat.history` in one flow.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing broadcast tests covered visible error delivery but not durable assistant transcript history.
- If no new test is added, why not: New regression assertions were added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Control UI users should now see the same failed assistant turn after refresh that they saw live when a started agent run fails before normal assistant output.

## Diagram

```text
real provider/runtime failure
  -> runner returns final failure payload marked isError
  -> Gateway returnedAgentErrorPayloads branch
  -> append one sanitized assistant error transcript entry with <runId>:assistant-error
  -> broadcast existing chat error terminal event
  -> reconnect chat.history still includes one assistant error
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No.
- Data access scope changed? No new scope; the change writes a sanitized assistant error to the same session transcript path already used for Gateway assistant persistence.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Not applicable.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux disposable test checkout
- Runtime/container: OpenClaw repository scripts with installed dependencies
- Model/provider: OpenAI-compatible provider configured to an unreachable local base URL for deterministic transport failure; no real credentials used
- Integration/channel (if any): Control UI-style Gateway WebSocket RPC; external channels skipped with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`
- Relevant config (redacted): Dummy redacted API key, local unreachable base URL, temporary state directory

### Steps

1. Start the real Gateway server from the patched checkout.
2. Connect a WebSocket client and send `chat.send` for a Control UI session.
3. Trigger a deterministic provider transport failure through the unreachable model base URL.
4. Wait for the run with `agent.wait`.
5. Read `chat.history` immediately.
6. Reconnect a fresh WebSocket client and read `chat.history` again.
7. Count assistant text messages and chat error events.

### Expected

- The run surfaces a live terminal error.
- Refreshed `chat.history` contains exactly one assistant error message for the failed run.
- The assistant error is not duplicated.

### Actual

- Before: refreshed `chat.history` had zero assistant messages for the failed run.
- After: refreshed `chat.history` had exactly one assistant error message and one chat error event.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

```text
Before real proof result: ASSISTANT_ERROR_HISTORY_MISMATCH
After real proof result: ONE_ASSISTANT_ERROR_IN_REFRESHED_HISTORY
After source proof: Gateway focused 3/3, Gateway adjacent 107/107, session guard 35/35, Runner/Followup focused 8/8.
TypeScript core check on the testserver did not produce diagnostics; the process was terminated by signal 9 at about 1.78 GB RSS, so this is reported as a testserver resource blocker rather than a green typecheck claim.
```

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: Returned agent-run error payload detection, runner final failure `isError` marking, Gateway assistant-error transcript append, runtime internal error-stub callback interaction, idempotency key behavior, real provider transport failure, immediate history, refreshed history after reconnect, and existing chat error event behavior.
- Edge cases checked: Runtime internal error stub does not suppress the Control UI-visible returned error transcript; Gateway writes one `<runId>:assistant-error` entry for the returned terminal error; no dependency or lockfile files changed.
- What you did **not** verify: Full repo-wide suite, successful `tsgo` core on the resource-limited testserver, real external provider credentials, screenshot/recording, and production channels.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No.
- Migration needed? No.
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: Not applicable.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: A runtime-internal error stub could suppress the Control UI-visible returned error transcript.
  - Mitigation: Returned terminal agent errors now force the Gateway assistant-error transcript append while retaining the existing idempotency key.
- Risk: Gateway could duplicate assistant errors.
  - Mitigation: The transcript append uses a stable `<runId>:assistant-error` idempotency key and the source tests cover the one-message behavior.
- Risk: Error text could bypass display sanitization.
  - Mitigation: The Gateway fallback still uses `sanitizeAssistantDisplayText` before transcript append.
- Risk: Dependency Guard could require security approval.
  - Mitigation: No dependency manifests, lockfiles, or dependency blocks were changed.

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts

The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key — so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.

target.title: fix(agents): honor disabled envelope timestamps at model boundary
target.body:
Opened on behalf of Onur Solmaz (`osolmaz`). AI-assisted contribution.

## Summary

OpenClaw could still send timestamp bytes to model input even when `agents.defaults.envelopeTimestamp` was `"off"`.
That matters for cache-sensitive local inference servers such as DS4, where users may want completely bare user-turn bytes.
This change routes the existing config through the direct timestamp helper and embedded LLM-boundary normalization while keeping timestamp injection enabled by default.
It also adds provider-payload tests showing that timestamps do not bust the reusable prompt prefix when timestamping stays enabled.

## What Changed

The existing config already disabled channel envelope timestamps, but two model-input paths did not read that setting.
This change makes those paths use the same timestamp inclusion decision.

- Added an `includeTimestamp` option to the gateway timestamp helper.
- Made `timestampOptsFromConfig()` set `includeTimestamp: false` when `agents.defaults.envelopeTimestamp` is `"off"`.
- Added the same toggle to embedded LLM-boundary normalization so current and historical user messages can stay bare for cache-sensitive runs.
- Passed the toggle through prompt precheck and hook normalization paths that render LLM-boundary message bytes.
- Added regression coverage for both the direct helper and LLM-boundary behavior.
- Added timestamp-on provider payload coverage for OpenAI Chat Completions and OpenAI Responses to verify that the stable prefix is byte-identical when a user turn moves from current to history.

## Compatibility And Migration

There is no persistent schema change and no migration is required.
The changed code only affects runtime prompt text construction and tests; it does not change stored transcript shape, database schema, cache storage files, package metadata, or migration code.

The compatibility change is intentional: `agents.defaults.envelopeTimestamp: "off"` now means timestamp text is off for the relevant model-input timestamp paths too, not only formatted channel envelopes.
The default behavior is unchanged because timestamping remains on unless this existing config key is explicitly set to `"off"`.
Users who set `envelopeTimestamp: "off"` and still depended on model-visible timestamp prefixes should remove that setting to keep timestamps.

## Cache Behavior

Turning timestamps off is not required for prompt-cache stability in the normal embedded path.
With timestamping enabled, OpenClaw stamps each user message from that message's stored timestamp, so the same old message serializes the same way when it is current and when it is replayed as history.
The provider-payload tests also assert that the next turn's timestamp appears after the stable prefix, not before it.

## Testing

I reproduced the disabled-timestamp issue first with a failing boundary test.
Before the fix, the new test received `[Wed 2024-06-05 07:01 UTC] Cache-sensitive current ask` when it expected bare text.
After the fix, the focused gateway, boundary, provider-payload, lint, format, and type checks pass.

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.cache-stability.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/agent-timestamp.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.cache-stability.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.llm-boundary.cache-stability.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`

I did not run a live DS4 cache-read measurement.
The added proof is source-level and provider-payload-level: it verifies the bytes OpenClaw builds before sending the model request.

## Risks

The default behavior is unchanged because timestamp injection still stays on unless config explicitly sets `envelopeTimestamp: "off"`.
The main risk is compatibility for users who disabled envelope timestamps but still relied on gateway-injected or LLM-boundary model timestamps; this change makes the config name behave more literally across model-input paths.

## Related Context

Related external discussion: https://github.com/antirez/ds4/pull/412

target.title: fix(ui): restore provider usage pill in desktop chat composer [AI]
target.body: ## Summary After 2026.6.6 the provider usage/quota pill (5h + weekly limits) disappeared from the desktop Control UI chat. The composer refactors (`feat: calm composer controls #88772`, `feat(webui): add session workspace rail #92856`) moved the desktop chat controls into `renderChatControls` but dropped the quota pill; the only desktop caller that still rendered it (the `renderChatSessionSelect` wrapper) became orphaned, so the pill rendered nowhere on desktop. Mobile was unaffected. This restores the **existing** pill (`renderChatQuotaPill`) into `renderChatControls` using the existing `models.authStatus` usage-window contract — provider-agnostic, no new config, no Codex/protocol change. It is intentionally scoped to re-attaching the existing pill, not the separately-tracked always-visible topbar. The pill now sits in the composer controls row (next to the model selector) rather than the pre-2026.6.6 upper-right header, since that desktop header no longer exists. Surface: `ui/` (Control UI desktop chat composer controls). Root cause / fix details: - `renderChatControls` (the shipped desktop composer controls) rendered the model select + settings popover but no longer the quota pill. - `renderGuardedChatControls` wraps `renderChatControls` in `guard([...])`; the dep list omitted `state.modelAuthStatusResult`, so even once the pill is re-attached the guarded controls would not re-render when usage windows arrive async. Added that dependency. - Desktop `.agent-chat__composer-controls` is flex (pill fits); the `@media (max-width: 768px)` layout is a fixed 2-column grid (model | settings), so the pill is hidden on mobile to avoid pushing the settings chip onto a second row. ## Linked context Refs #93041 ## Real behavior proof - **Behavior addressed:** Desktop Control UI chat no longer shows the OpenAI/Codex (or any monitored provider) usage pill after 2026.6.6; this restores it and makes it update when `models.authStatus` usage windows arrive. - **Real environment tested:** The production Control UI, served by the mocked-gateway harness and rendered in **real headless Chromium 148** (Playwright) at a 1280×900 desktop viewport — the full app on the real `/chat` route, not jsdom. `models.authStatus` returned an OAuth Codex provider with 5h + Week usage windows. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui-e2e.config.ts --configLoader runner ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-quota-pill-93041.e2e.test.ts` - **Evidence after fix:** Headless Chromium screenshot of the production Control UI desktop chat — the composer controls row shows the restored provider pill reading **"Usage 29%"**, linking to `/usage`, between the model selector and the Chat settings button. - **Observed result after fix:** The pill renders **"Usage 29%"** in the desktop composer controls and links to `/usage`; it is absent when no provider usage windows are present. On `origin/main`, the identical harness rendered no pill even with usage windows present. - **What was not tested:** No live in-browser session against a real ChatGPT/Codex OAuth subscription (the gateway `models.authStatus` was mocked with representative OAuth usage windows). Mobile intentionally keeps no composer pill (hidden via CSS to preserve the 2-column mobile grid). ## Tests and validation - `ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-quota-pill-93041.e2e.test.ts` — **real-browser (Playwright/Chromium)** test: mocks `models.authStatus` usage windows, asserts the pill renders in `.agent-chat__composer-controls` on `/chat`, and that it is absent without usage windows. Skips gracefully when Chromium is unavailable. 2/2 pass. - `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.browser.test.ts` — added 2 jsdom tests asserting the pill renders in `renderChatControls` with usage data and is absent without it (guards the live surface the existing `chat.test.ts` test missed — it rendered the orphaned wrapper). 9/9 pass. - `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` — 104/104, unchanged. - `oxfmt --check`, `oxlint`, `tsgo:core`, `tsgo:test:ui` — all clean. ## Risk Low, UI-only. Reuses an existing render helper and the existing `models.authStatus` projection; no config/default/migration/auth-logic change. ## AI assistance AI-assisted (Claude). Investigation, fix, and proof were reviewed and are owned by me.

target.title: [AI-assisted] Persist agent-run error replies in chat history
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: A started Control UI agent run could surface a terminal provider/runtime error live, but `chat.history` after refresh did not retain an assistant error turn for that failed run.
- Solution: Treat agent-run failure replies as real error payloads and make Gateway persist exactly one display-safe assistant error transcript entry for returned agent errors, even when the runtime emitted only an internal error stub.
- What changed: Runner failure payloads are marked `isError`, Gateway appends a deduped `<runId>:assistant-error` transcript entry for returned agent errors, and regression coverage now locks the returned-error, runtime-stub, and duplicate/idempotency paths.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No provider behavior changes, no delivery routing changes, no config/lifecycle changes, no raw transcript writes, and no dependency or lockfile changes.

## Motivation

- A user should not lose the visible assistant error after refreshing the Control UI. If a started run fails before normal assistant output, the live error event and durable history should agree.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #89197
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: A real Gateway/WebSocket Control UI run that fails at provider transport now leaves exactly one assistant error in refreshed `chat.history`.
- Real environment tested: Disposable Linux OpenClaw checkout with the patched PR branch, real Gateway server startup, real WebSocket RPC client, channels skipped with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, and an intentionally unreachable OpenAI-compatible base URL to trigger the provider failure without secrets.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts -- -t 'assistant error|returned agent-run error payloads|runtime emitted an internal error stub'
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.error-broadcast.test.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts -- -t 'Agent failed before reply|assistant error|fallback candidate|error stub'
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node --import tsx ../real-chat-history-proof.ts after-gateway-force-v3
/usr/bin/time -v node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
Real runtime proof:
- provider transport failed with fetch failed / Connection error
- agent.wait status: error
- immediateHistoryCount: 3
- refreshHistoryCount: 3
- immediateAssistantCount: 1
- refreshAssistantCount: 1
- chatErrorEventCount: 1
- result: ONE_ASSISTANT_ERROR_IN_REFRESHED_HISTORY

Focused Gateway proof: 3 passed, 102 skipped.
Adjacent Gateway proof: 107 passed.
Session tool result guard proof: 35 passed.
Runner/Followup focused proof: 8 passed, 214 skipped.
git diff --check: clean.
```

- Observed result after fix: The live provider failure still broadcasts the expected chat error, and both immediate and post-reconnect `chat.history` contain one assistant error text: `Agent failed before reply: LLM request failed: network connection error.`
- What was not tested: Full repository-wide test suite, successful `tsgo` core on this testserver, real provider credentials, and a browser screenshot.
- Before evidence:

```text
Unpatched main proof for the same real Gateway/WebSocket/provider-failure path:
- sendStatus: started
- agent.wait status: ok
- immediateHistoryCount: 2
- refreshHistoryCount: 2
- immediateAssistantCount: 0
- refreshAssistantCount: 0
- chatErrorEventCount: 0
- result: ASSISTANT_ERROR_HISTORY_MISMATCH
```

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Runner final failure replies were not consistently marked as error payloads, and Gateway treated the runtime assistant-error callback as if it guaranteed a Control UI-visible `chat.history` assistant turn.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Previous coverage asserted live error broadcast behavior but did not prove refreshed `chat.history` retained an assistant error after a started real runtime failure.
- Contributing context (if known): Runtime/session guard error stubs can be internal transcript-only markers; Gateway still needs to durably mirror returned terminal agent errors into the Control UI history path.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`, and the temporary real Gateway/WebSocket proof harness.
- Scenario the test should lock in: A started run returns an agent error; Gateway appends one assistant error transcript entry, keeps the live error terminal event, survives reconnect/history refresh, and does not duplicate the assistant error.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The source tests cover the exact Gateway and runner seams, while the real proof exercises server startup, WebSocket RPC, provider transport failure, `agent.wait`, and refreshed `chat.history` in one flow.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing broadcast tests covered visible error delivery but not durable assistant transcript history.
- If no new test is added, why not: New regression assertions were added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Control UI users should now see the same failed assistant turn after refresh that they saw live when a started agent run fails before normal assistant output.

## Diagram

```text
real provider/runtime failure
  -> runner returns final failure payload marked isError
  -> Gateway returnedAgentErrorPayloads branch
  -> append one sanitized assistant error transcript entry with <runId>:assistant-error
  -> broadcast existing chat error terminal event
  -> reconnect chat.history still includes one assistant error
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No.
- Data access scope changed? No new scope; the change writes a sanitized assistant error to the same session transcript path already used for Gateway assistant persistence.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Not applicable.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux disposable test checkout
- Runtime/container: OpenClaw repository scripts with installed dependencies
- Model/provider: OpenAI-compatible provider configured to an unreachable local base URL for deterministic transport failure; no real credentials used
- Integration/channel (if any): Control UI-style Gateway WebSocket RPC; external channels skipped with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`
- Relevant config (redacted): Dummy redacted API key, local unreachable base URL, temporary state directory

### Steps

1. Start the real Gateway server from the patched checkout.
2. Connect a WebSocket client and send `chat.send` for a Control UI session.
3. Trigger a deterministic provider transport failure through the unreachable model base URL.
4. Wait for the run with `agent.wait`.
5. Read `chat.history` immediately.
6. Reconnect a fresh WebSocket client and read `chat.history` again.
7. Count assistant text messages and chat error events.

### Expected

- The run surfaces a live terminal error.
- Refreshed `chat.history` contains exactly one assistant error message for the failed run.
- The assistant error is not duplicated.

### Actual

- Before: refreshed `chat.history` had zero assistant messages for the failed run.
- After: refreshed `chat.history` had exactly one assistant error message and one chat error event.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

```text
Before real proof result: ASSISTANT_ERROR_HISTORY_MISMATCH
After real proof result: ONE_ASSISTANT_ERROR_IN_REFRESHED_HISTORY
After source proof: Gateway focused 3/3, Gateway adjacent 107/107, session guard 35/35, Runner/Followup focused 8/8.
TypeScript core check on the testserver did not produce diagnostics; the process was terminated by signal 9 at about 1.78 GB RSS, so this is reported as a testserver resource blocker rather than a green typecheck claim.
```

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: Returned agent-run error payload detection, runner final failure `isError` marking, Gateway assistant-error transcript append, runtime internal error-stub callback interaction, idempotency key behavior, real provider transport failure, immediate history, refreshed history after reconnect, and existing chat error event behavior.
- Edge cases checked: Runtime internal error stub does not suppress the Control UI-visible returned error transcript; Gateway writes one `<runId>:assistant-error` entry for the returned terminal error; no dependency or lockfile files changed.
- What you did **not** verify: Full repo-wide suite, successful `tsgo` core on the resource-limited testserver, real external provider credentials, screenshot/recording, and production channels.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No.
- Migration needed? No.
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: Not applicable.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: A runtime-internal error stub could suppress the Control UI-visible returned error transcript.
  - Mitigation: Returned terminal agent errors now force the Gateway assistant-error transcript append while retaining the existing idempotency key.
- Risk: Gateway could duplicate assistant errors.
  - Mitigation: The transcript append uses a stable `<runId>:assistant-error` idempotency key and the source tests cover the one-message behavior.
- Risk: Error text could bypass display sanitization.
  - Mitigation: The Gateway fallback still uses `sanitizeAssistantDisplayText` before transcript append.
- Risk: Dependency Guard could require security approval.
  - Mitigation: No dependency manifests, lockfiles, or dependency blocks were changed.

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches
target.author: alaleiwang
